title: DataLost variant is the correct contract for non-atomic NVM overwrite
description: nvm.rs: nvm_overwrite deletes the existing object then adds the new one. The wolfHSM NVM protocol has no rollback facility, so if the add fails after a successful delete the original data is permanently gone. DataLost{id} encodes this in the type system: callers that pattern-match on WolfHsmError must handle the lost-data case explicitly. A future contributor might collapse DataLost into Wh or Ffi to reduce enum size. That is wrong: callers need to distinguish whether data is still intact, and the id field names exactly which object needs recreation.
